function.name: search_wikipedia
function.description: Search the Wikipedia with given query. The `key` field in the response is the Wikipedia page name. Uses network.

query.description: The search terms in the language matching the second parameter.

language.description: Language code matching the search query. For example, `en` for English or `pl` for Polish.

function.name: get_wikipedia
function.description: Retrieve the Wikipedia article on given subject. Uses network.

function.name: get_dictionary
function.description: Retrieve description of a word from dictionary. Uses network.

function.name: search_web
function.description: Search the web with given query. Uses network.

function.name: get_webpage
function.description: Fetch contents of a web page at given URL. Uses network.

function.name: user_manual
function.description: Search the Tracy Profiler user manual with given query.

query.description: Verbose search query in English language.

function.name: source_file
function.description: Retrieve the user program source file contents around given line.

context.description: How many lines to get after the target line. Default: 2.

context_back.description: How many lines to get before the target line. Default: 2.

function.name: source_search
function.description: Search user program source files for given keyword.

function.name: get_slow_frames
function.description: Find frames that are unusually slow. Returns frame numbers, their duration in milliseconds, and how many times slower they are than the average. Use this to identify which specific frames are causing hitches or stutter.

threshold_ms.description: Report frames slower than this many milliseconds. If omitted, returns the slowest frames automatically.

top_n.description: Maximum number of slow frames to return (default: 20).

function.name: get_frame_zones
function.description: Get a breakdown of all instrumented zones (function calls) that executed during a specific frame, grouped by thread. Shows each zone's name, source location, duration and percentage of frame time. Use this after identifying a slow frame with get_slow_frames to find out what was taking the most time. IMPORTANT: only works on completed (disconnected) recordings, not on live sessions.

frame.description: Frame number to analyze (as returned by get_slow_frames).

min_pct.description: Only show zones that take at least this percentage of the frame time (default: 1.0). Lower values show more detail.

max_depth.description: Maximum depth of the zone call tree to expand (default: 6).

function.name: get_frame_hotspots_with_callstack
function.description: Analyze one frame and return top hotspots with per-hotspot callstack snippets. Use this to quickly identify what was expensive and where it was called from. Only works on completed (disconnected) recordings.

top_n.description: Maximum number of hotspots to return (default: 10).

min_pct.description: Only include hotspots that take at least this percentage of frame time (default: 1.0).

max_stack_depth.description: Maximum stack frames to include per hotspot (default: 8).
